skill.name: search-specialist
skill.description: Guides advanced web research, source filtering, fact checking, competitive analysis, and multi-source synthesis for deep information gathering.
skill.summary: Guides web research with search operators, source checks, and synthesized findings.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported possible shell execution at SKILL.md:26 and weak cryptography at SKILL.md:3. Both findings are false positives: line 26 is a Markdown reference to a resource file, and line 3 is descriptive text with no cryptographic operation. No prompt injection, exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed skill instructions.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Markdown File Reference
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:26 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The backticks are Markdown formatting around a resource path, not executable code, and no command invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains a natural language instruction to open a Markdown resource path. There is no shell syntax, interpreter call, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: No Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:3 as a weak cryptographic algorithm. That line is part of the skill description and contains no cryptographic primitive, hashing function, or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain metadata text describing web research capabilities. No code or crypto-related behavior exists at the reported location.

content.user_title: Research Web Sources With Better Search Strategy
content.value_statement: Many research tasks fail because searches are too broad, sources are weak, or claims are not verified. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan searches, compare sources, and synthesize findings.
actual_capabilities: Creates multiple search query variations for a research objective.
Uses domain filtering, exact phrases, exclusions, and timeframe constraints.
Evaluates source quality and compares claims across sources.
Synthesizes findings into concise research summaries.
Identifies contradictions, gaps, and follow-up research needs.
Supports competitive analysis, fact checking, and trend research.
limitations: It depends on the host assistant having access to web search or page retrieval tools.
It does not guarantee that every source is current or complete.
It cannot verify private, paywalled, or unavailable information without provided access.
It provides research guidance, not legal, medical, or financial advice.

use_cases.title: Validate Product Claims
use_cases.description: Compare public sources, find supporting evidence, and flag unsupported claims before publishing.

use_cases.title: Research Market Competitors
use_cases.description: Find competitor positioning, pricing signals, product changes, and public customer feedback.

use_cases.title: Check Emerging Trends
use_cases.description: Search recent sources, separate signal from repetition, and summarize reliable trend evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Fact Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner research task with one claim.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the search-specialist skill to verify this claim: [claim]. Find at least three reliable sources and summarize whether the claim is supported.

prompt_templates.title: Source Comparison
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare several public sources for consistency.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the search-specialist skill to research [topic]. Compare source quality, note contradictions, and provide the strongest supported conclusion.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the search-specialist skill to research competitors for [product or company]. Track positioning, pricing signals, key features, and recent changes.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Research Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Produce a structured brief with methodology.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the search-specialist skill to create a research brief on [topic]. Include search queries used, source credibility, verified findings, gaps, contradictions, and next research steps.

output_examples.input: Research whether a vendor's security certification claim is accurate.
output_examples.output: A short verification brief with the vendor claim, sources checked, supporting evidence, unresolved gaps, and a confidence rating.

output_examples.input: Compare three project management tools for a buying decision.
output_examples.output: A structured comparison of positioning, features, pricing evidence, review patterns, source quality, and recommended next checks.

output_examples.input: Find recent evidence for a market trend.
output_examples.output: A trend summary that separates recent primary sources, secondary commentary, repeated claims, contradictions, and open questions.

best_practices: State the research goal, timeframe, geography, and source requirements before searching.
Use multiple query variations and compare results across independent sources.
Separate verified facts, source interpretations, and unresolved gaps in the final answer.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on one search result or one source for important claims.
Do not treat repeated articles as independent confirmation without checking origin.
Do not omit source URLs or credibility notes from research summaries.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan web research, refine searches, evaluate sources, verify claims, and synthesize findings.

faq.question: Does it perform web searches by itself?
faq.answer: It provides the research workflow. Actual searching depends on the tools available to Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it handle competitive analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide research into competitors, positioning, feature evidence, pricing signals, and public market activity.

faq.question: How does it improve fact checking?
faq.answer: It encourages multiple queries, source comparison, credibility assessment, and clear separation of evidence from assumptions.

faq.question: Is it useful for academic research?
faq.answer: It can help find and compare sources, but users should still follow academic citation and database requirements.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the topic, target audience, timeframe, geography, preferred source types, and any sources to include or avoid.
